name: Economic Analysis Basics
description: Cost-benefit reasoning, economic surplus, opportunity cost, and marginal vs. average analysis.

name: Comparative Advantage, PPCs, and Trade
description: Absolute and comparative advantage, production possibilities curves, and gains from specialization and trade.

name: Supply, Demand, and Market Equilibrium
description: Demand and supply curves, market equilibrium, causes of shifts, and market surplus.

name: GDP Accounting
description: The expenditure, income, and value-added approaches to measuring GDP, and common accounting traps.

name: Nominal GDP, Real GDP, CPI, and Inflation
description: Real vs. nominal GDP, the CPI, inflation, real interest rates, indexing, and CPI measurement bias.

name: Labor Markets
description: Labor demand, the value of marginal product, labor supply, reservation wages, and shifts in labor markets.

name: Productivity and Real Wages
description: The link between productivity and wage growth, the post-1970 slowdown, and the GDP-per-capita decomposition.

name: Inequality, Globalization, and Skill-Biased Technological Change
description: How globalization and skill-biased technological change affect wage inequality, and policy responses.

name: Economic Growth
description: The story of modern economic growth, cross-country divergence, poverty traps, and the limits of growth.

name: Growth Accounting, Compound Growth, and Rule of 72
description: Compound growth formulas, the Rule of 72, and growth-rate accounting calculations.

name: Sources of Growth: Capital, Technology, Human Capital, Institutions
description: The determinants of average labor productivity, including capital, technology, human capital, and institutions.
